title: Eros Health Index
subtitle: A transparent prototype for tracking embodied connection, synthetic substitution, relational reciprocity, and generativity.

metrics.label: Adults living with spouse or partner
metrics.note: Derived as 100 minus the reported 42% living without a spouse or partner.

metrics.label: Unmarried young adults who want to marry
metrics.note: Intent is not behavior, so this is weighted below actual partnership.

metrics.label: 12th graders expecting to marry someday
metrics.source_name: Pew Research Center / Monitoring the Future
metrics.note: Tracks expectation of durable pair-bonding among older adolescents.

accountability_split.rationale: Orientation signal with a reported sex gap: 2023 negative pressure is split from 26% of boys versus 39% of girls not saying they expect to marry. This is a directional proxy, not a blame allocation.

metrics.label: Teens using AI companions at least monthly
metrics.note: Broad usage metric; not every use is romantic or isolating.

accountability_split.rationale: Synthetic relational substitution is mapped to the GOONER behavioral archetype regardless of user sex.

metrics.label: Teens rating AI companion talk as equally or more satisfying than real friends
metrics.note: Closer to substitution risk than simple trial or occasional use.

accountability_split.rationale: Synthetic relational substitution is mapped to the GOONER behavioral archetype regardless of user sex.

metrics.label: Teens online almost constantly
metrics.note: A proxy for digital saturation, not a direct measure of misdirected eros.

metrics.label: Marriage rate
metrics.note: Annual vital-statistics signal. The updater can refresh this page value.

metrics.label: General fertility rate
metrics.note: Generativity signal. It should not be read as a judgment of individual childlessness.

metrics.label: Youth exposed to online pornography before 18
metrics.note: Shown as international context but excluded from the US headline composite.

metrics.label: Young men favoring harder access to online pornography
metrics.note: Attitude indicator shown as a possible cultural antibody, not an outcome metric.

metrics.label: People socializing or communicating on an average day
metrics.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, American Time Use Survey
metrics.note: Direct embodied-socialization outcome. BLS reports 30% in 2025 versus 38% in 2015. The 2025 release also carries a shutdown-related diary-gap and reweighting caveat; this outcome stays neutral in the accountability meter.

metrics.label: Online daters receiving unwanted sexually explicit content
metrics.note: Direct adverse-experience signal among people who have used online dating. This is victim-experience prevalence, not perpetrator prevalence; one actor can affect multiple users. Pew also reports 30% experienced continued contact after saying they were not interested, so the two correlated harms are not double-counted here.

accountability_split.rationale: Unsolicited sexual content is mapped to desire severed from reciprocal relationship. The bucket describes the behavior, not the sex of the actor.

signal_roles.outcome: Observed state of embodied connection, relationship quality, family formation, or social life.
signal_roles.orientation: Stated desire or expectation that may precede behavior but is not itself an outcome.
signal_roles.impairment: Direct harm, loss of control, relationship interference, or adverse experience.
signal_roles.exposure_proxy: Population exposure or use that may create pressure but does not establish pathology.
signal_roles.mechanism: Evidence about a plausible behavioral process; usually not a national trend by itself.
signal_roles.context_only: Relevant context shown without headline weight.
signal_roles.countercurrent: Potential protective attitude or behavioral countertrend shown without headline weight.

evidence_tiers.A: Recurring nationally representative government or similarly rigorous national data.
evidence_tiers.B: High-quality nationally representative snapshot or slower-moving survey anchor.
evidence_tiers.C: Peer-reviewed experimental, longitudinal, or observational mechanism evidence.
evidence_tiers.WATCHLIST: Plausible and relevant, but not strong or repeatable enough to score.

methodology.accountability_rule: Only metrics explicitly marked accountability_include participate. Each archetype receives an independently normalized weighted severity score after signal-role, evidence-tier, and mapping-confidence modifiers. The public split compares those side severities rather than dividing one shared pressure denominator. Population outcomes remain neutral unless a defensible directional behavioral mapping exists. Comparisons are provisional until each side has at least two independent clusters and one impairment signal.

research_pipeline.reason: Requires WTSSPS-weighted extraction and a documented rule for combining married and cohabiting respondents. Raw codebook counts are intentionally not used as national estimates.
Candidate thresholds include weekly-or-more and a graded frequency score. Frequency will remain an exposure proxy, not a diagnosis of problematic use.
Sexual inactivity is not automatically dysfunction. Analyze by age and relationship status before deciding whether any aggregate belongs in the headline index.

research_watchlist.label: Fantasy-romance and erotic-fiction displacement
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Sales or readership measure exposure, not impairment or displacement of real relationships.

research_watchlist.label: Deceptive filters and strategic dating-profile presentation
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Relevant to trust and mate-market distortion, but no clean recurring national series currently supports headline weight.

research_watchlist.label: Breadcrumbing, benching, and attention extraction
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Conceptually strong reciprocity failure; prevalence evidence is not yet nationally repeatable enough for scoring.

research_watchlist.label: Catalogization and dating choice overload
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: National surveys and experiments support the mechanism, but no recurring impairment-sensitive national series currently earns score weight.

research_watchlist.label: Relationship-specific relational aggression
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Real behavioral literature, but adult pair-bond relevance and sex attribution are too heterogeneous for a clean dashboard metric.

research_watchlist.label: Coparental gatekeeping
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Family-cohesion relevance is high; causal interpretation and repeatable national measurement are not clean enough for scoring.

research_watchlist.label: Financial infidelity and secret relationship-relevant behavior
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: A clear reciprocity concern, but existing measurement is not yet suitable as a recurring national EHI series.

research_watchlist.label: Paid parasocial and erotic attention markets
research_watchlist.reason_not_scored: Revenue and account counts do not establish personal impairment or displacement of embodied relationships.
